question: Design a `NotificationSystem` class. Overload its constructor to accept different notification types (e.g., `enum NotificationType { SMS, EMAIL, PUSH }`). Also, overload a `send` method to handle sending messages of different content types (e.g., `std::string`, `int`).

test_cases.input: Create SMS notifier and send string message
test_cases.expected: Sending SMS notification: Hello via SMS!

test_cases.input: Create EMAIL notifier and send int message (12345)
test_cases.expected: Sending EMAIL notification: Generic integer message

test_cases.input: Create PUSH notifier and send string message
Create PUSH notifier and send int message (54321)
test_cases.expected: Sending PUSH notification: Generic integer message

errors: The 'send(int messageId)' method has a logical error: it prints a generic string 'Generic integer message' instead of the actual 'messageId' value.
